senses.definition: an institute that teaches people how to drive
the numbers and letters on the plates at the front and back of a vehicle
any type of vehicle that is powered by an engine
a bar that is attached to the back and front of a vehicle to reduce damage in time of an accident
a brake that is operated by hand to hold a vehicle in place

examples.x: He pulled the emergency brake to stop the car from rolling down the hill.
The driver engaged the emergency brake before exiting the parked car.
In case of brake failure, the emergency brake can be used to slow down the vehicle safely.
The mechanic adjusted the tension on the emergency brake cable to ensure it functions properly.
It's important to release the emergency brake before driving to avoid damaging the braking system.

senses.definition: a metal part that covers the engine of a vehicle

examples.x: He popped the hood of the car to check the engine after it started making strange noises.
The mechanic lifted the hood to inspect the damage from the minor accident.
She slammed the hood shut after filling up the windshield washer fluid.
A layer of dust had accumulated on the hood, indicating the car hadn't been driven in months.
The hood of the sports car gleamed under the showroom lights, showcasing its pristine condition.

senses.definition: a flat piece of metal at the front and back of a vehicle displaying numbers and letters

examples.x: She customized her car's plate with a personalized message for a unique touch.
He checked the plate to ensure it matched the registration paperwork before buying the car.
The vehicle's plate was damaged, so he went to the DMV to get a replacement.
The plate was covered in mud, making it hard to read the numbers during the off-road trip.
The security guard recorded the plate of the car entering the restricted area for access control.

senses.definition: a strap that keeps a person attached to their seat to prevent injuries, especially in cars, etc.

examples.x: She fastened her safety belt before starting the car to ensure she was protected in case of an accident.
He reminded his passengers to buckle up and use their safety belts before pulling out of the driveway.
They checked that the safety belts were functioning properly as part of the vehicle’s safety inspection.
She felt more secure driving after installing new safety belts in her older vehicle.

senses.definition: a pipe through which harmful gasses exit from a car

examples.x: The mechanic examined the tailpipe for signs of exhaust leaks or corrosion.
He noticed black smoke coming from the tailpipe, indicating a potential engine problem.
The car emitted a loud noise from the tailpipe, suggesting an issue with the muffler.
The tailpipe emissions are regulated to reduce air pollution and improve air quality.
She installed a new chrome-plated tailpipe to enhance the appearance of her vehicle.

senses.definition: a container that holds the fuel of a vehicle, etc.
a circular rubber object that covers the wheel of a vehicle
the space at the back of an automobile in which different things can be put
a light on a vehicle that blinks to indicate a change in lane
the large front window of a vehicle
a long and thin device with rubber on its edge, designed to move across the glass at the front of vehicles to clear it of rain, snow, etc. so that the driver can see the road properly

examples.x: She turned on the windshield wiper to clear the heavy rain from the windshield during the storm.
He adjusted the speed of the windshield wiper to match the intensity of the falling snow.
She used the windshield wiper to remove the morning frost from the windshield before starting her commute.
The windshield wiper squeaked as it moved across the glass, indicating it needed some maintenance.

senses.definition: an accident in which a vehicle, plane, etc. hits something else

examples.x: The crash on the highway caused a major traffic delay and required emergency response.
She witnessed a crash between two cars at the intersection and immediately called for help.
The report detailed the causes of the crash and the extent of the damage to the vehicles involved.
He was shaken but unharmed after the crash that occurred when he lost control of his car.

senses.definition: a curve in a road, river, etc.

examples.x: The car approached a sharp bend in the road and slowed down.
The river took a meandering bend through the picturesque valley.

senses.definition: the place where two streets or roads cross each other

examples.x: The traffic light turned green, allowing vehicles to proceed through the busy crossing.
He waited patiently at the crossing for the oncoming traffic to clear before making a left turn.
The city installed a roundabout at the crossing to improve traffic flow and reduce accidents.
The police officer directed traffic at the crossing during rush hour to manage congestion effectively.

senses.definition: the place where two or more streets, roads, etc. cross each other

examples.x: The traffic light at the intersection changed to red, stopping cars from all directions.
They waited for the green light at the busy intersection to continue their journey.
The city planned to redesign the intersection to improve safety and reduce congestion.
He noticed a new restaurant opening at the corner of the intersection where several roads converged.
She was involved in a minor accident at the intersection due to another driver running a red light.

senses.definition: a turn that a car, etc. makes to move toward the direction it was coming from

examples.x: He made a U-turn in the intersection after realizing he had missed his destination.
The GPS instructed her to make a U-turn when she missed the exit on the highway.
They performed a U-turn in the parking lot to find a better spot closer to the store entrance.
She carefully executed a U-turn on the narrow street to head back home.

senses.definition: a satellite system that shows a place, thing, or person's exact position using signals
a time of day at which traffic is the heaviest because people are leaving for work or home

examples.x: She left the house early to avoid the rush hour traffic on her way to the office.
The highway was congested during rush hour, causing a long delay for commuters.
The local news reported on the construction project affecting rush hour traffic patterns.
They decided to take public transportation to avoid the stress of driving during rush hour.
She planned her errands around rush hour to avoid getting stuck in traffic.

senses.definition: the traffic offence of driving faster than is legally allowed

examples.x: He was fined for his repeated offenses of speeding and had to attend a driving course.
The police crackdown on speeding aimed to improve safety on the roads.
The government launched a campaign to raise awareness about the dangers of speeding.

senses.definition: to slow down or stop a moving car, etc. by using the brakes

examples.x: The cyclist had to quickly brake to avoid colliding with the pedestrian crossing the street.
As the train approached the station, the engineer began to gradually brake to ensure a gentle and precise stop.
The skilled skater knew exactly when to brake, gracefully slowing down.
The instructor taught the new driver how to properly brake in various situations.
In heavy traffic, it's essential to maintain a safe following distance and be prepared to brake quickly if needed.

senses.definition: to leave a place, vehicle, etc.

examples.x: After the movie ended, the audience began to exit the theater.
Upon reaching their destination, the weary travelers were eager to exit the plane.
The passengers patiently waited for the train to come to a complete stop before starting to exit the subway car.
In case of a fire drill, employees are instructed to calmly exit the building .

senses.definition: (of a vehicle) to come to a stop

examples.x: The limousine pulled up, and a famous celebrity stepped out.
The delivery van pulled up outside the bakery early in the morning.
Just as I was thinking of leaving, her bike pulled up outside the cafe.

senses.definition: to choose the direction of and guide a vehicle, ship, etc., especially by using a map

examples.x: She helped the driver navigate by providing turn-by-turn directions.
The travel agent provided a detailed itinerary, helping the vacationers navigate through scenic routes.
The driving instructor taught the student how to navigate through traffic and use road signs.
Using a paper map, the backpackers took turns navigating.
The navigator instructed the driver on how to navigate through diverse landscapes and terrains.

senses.definition: to move or act very quickly

examples.x: When the fire alarm sounded, the students had to rush out of the building in an orderly manner.
Realizing he was late for the meeting, John had to rush to the conference room.
The mother had to rush to the store to buy groceries before it closed.
Seeing the approaching storm, the beachgoers decided to rush back to their cars.
To catch the last bus, the passengers had to rush to the bus stop.

senses.definition: to decrease the speed of something

examples.x: The driver slowed the car as they approached the intersection.
She slowed the treadmill to a walking pace after an intense workout.
The cyclist slowed his bike to let the pedestrians cross the road.
The pilot slowed the aircraft during the descent for a smooth landing.
The technician slowed the conveyor belt to avoid jamming the production line.

senses.definition: to provide energy or power for a vehicle, etc.

examples.x: He fueled the car by adding gasoline at the gas station.
She used a can of propane to fuel the grill for the outdoor barbecue.
He needs to fuel his motorcycle before heading out on the long ride.
They fueled the rocket with a mixture of liquid hydrogen and oxygen before the launch.
She fueled the car's tank before the road trip to ensure they wouldn't run out of gas.

senses.definition: forward movement or movement toward somewhere

examples.x: The GPS showed their progress as they traveled along the highway.
She was pleased with the progress made during the road trip, covering a large distance each day.
The driver checked the map to track their progress through the unfamiliar territory.
Despite the heavy rain, they made steady progress on their journey to the mountains.

senses.definition: the wheel that a driver holds or turns to make a vehicle move in different directions

examples.x: She adjusted the steering wheel to a comfortable position before starting her drive.
He turned the steering wheel sharply to avoid the obstacle in the road.
The car's steering wheel was equipped with controls for audio and cruise settings.
She noticed a problem with the steering wheel when it became difficult to turn while driving.
He gripped the steering wheel tightly as he navigated through the slippery conditions.

senses.definition: a bar in front of a motorcycle or bicycle that a person takes by hand to control the direction in which they want to travel

examples.x: He adjusted the handlebar height to make his bike more comfortable to ride.
She steered the bicycle by turning the handlebar left and right while navigating the path.
The mechanic repaired the broken handlebar on the motorcycle to ensure it was safe to ride.
They added grips to the handlebar for better control and comfort during long rides.
He noticed a slight wobble in the handlebar, which he had to fix before his next ride.

senses.definition: an area on a road that is marked with wide white lines, where vehicles must stop so people could walk across the road safely

examples.x: She waited at the zebra crossing until the traffic stopped, allowing her to cross the street safely.
The city installed new zebra crossings near the school to improve safety for students.
He used the zebra crossing to navigate across the busy intersection during rush hour.
The driver slowed down as soon as he saw the zebra crossing, anticipating pedestrians.
They painted the zebra crossing with bright, reflective paint to increase visibility at night.

senses.definition: a handle in a car or other vehicle, by which a driver can change gears
the red light at the back of a vehicle that lits up when brakes are used to signal stopping or slowing down to other drivers
an aggressive behavior that is seen among drivers, particularly when they are stuck in traffic

examples.x: He experienced road rage when another driver cut him off and responded with angry gestures.
The incident of road rage escalated when the two drivers started shouting at each other at the traffic light.
She tried to avoid road rage by practicing deep breathing and staying calm during traffic jams.
The news reported a case of road rage where one driver threw objects at another vehicle.
The driving instructor emphasized the importance of avoiding road rage and maintaining composure on the road.
